title: Connecting monday.com to PowerApps
excerpt:
Hi I am having trouble completing the setup of a Custom Connector from Microsoft PowerApps to monday.com.  My problem is that I get a Response of (200) "“message”: “No query string was present”. 
I have tested that I can…

posts.author: bungle (nick pearson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi I am having trouble completing the setup of a Custom Connector from Microsoft PowerApps to monday.com. My problem is that I get a Response of (200) "“message”: “No query string was present”.

I have tested that I can access the API by running a test through postman and I get a good response and data back. So I know I can connect. I have then setup the Customer Connector as follows:

GENERAL TAB
Scheme = HTTPS
Host = api.monday.com
Base URL = /v2

SECURITY TAB
Authentication Type = API Key
Parameter Label = API Key
Parameter Names = Authorization
Parameter Location = Header

Definition Tab
I have supplied a screen shot for this

I have setup the expected Response as “account_id”, “id” and "name’ which are all part of the basic query I have defaulted in the body of the request and also in the postman test (which works fine). It looks like something is not quite right with the Custom Connector as monday.com responses consistently says there is no Query but you can see the query being sent by the Custom Connector API. Wondered if you could help please? Screen shots are attached.

Test - Request987×702 81.7 KB
Test - Response942×653 49.2 KB
Setup - Default Response Columns897×486 45.5 KB
Setup - Request Body846×550 25.4 KB
Setup - Request General880×410 41.1 KB
Postman example test1105×541 46.2 KB

posts.author: bungle (nick pearson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I recently found this article in relation to the above that seems to state the GraphQL needs to be provided within a RESTful API from monday.com - not sure if the API is fully RESTful?
Consuming GraphQL based API in PowerApps - Power Platform Community

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @bungle,

Ahh great question! Yes indeed-- I’m afraid that our API is indeed GraphQL and not RESTful.

This is probably why you’re experiencing this odd error message.

Would it be possible for you to continue utilizing Postman (and possibly creating your own backend) to accomplish your workflow?

-Helen

posts.author: bungle (nick pearson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Helen

Not really as I wanted to expose the data from Monday via MS PowerApps and across our company to expand the availability of information and utilise the corporate communications setup within Sharepoint.

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @bungle,

Makes sense-- maybe it might make more sense to utilize something like Zapier or Integromat then, as a third-party integration platform that would allow you to link together monday.com and Sharepoint!

-Helen

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
